name: Find and fix a memory leak in an event system
task: /tmp/eval_leak/bus.py has a memory leak: do_work() subscribes a handler each call but unsubscribe() is a no-op stub, so the _handlers list grows unbounded across many calls. Fix EventBus.unsubscribe so it actually removes the handler from the per-event list. After your fix: calling do_work(bus, i) 10000 times must leave bus._handlers['tick'] with length 0 (or the key absent). Do not change the public API or the do_work function.

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_leak/bus.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); bus = mod.EventBus(); [mod.do_work(bus, i) for i in range(10000)]; remaining = len(bus._handlers.get('tick', [])); assert remaining == 0, f'leak not fixed: {remaining} handlers still attached after 10000 calls'; bus2 = mod.EventBus(); marks = []; h = bus2.subscribe('x', lambda d: marks.append(d)); bus2.publish('x', 1); assert marks == [1]; bus2.unsubscribe('x', h); bus2.publish('x', 2); assert marks == [1], 'unsubscribe should stop delivery'; print('OK')
